mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over consecutive FOMC meetings raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the tightening cycle continues.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.78% in Oct 2005, up from ~1% in 2004 amid ongoing Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts positive mean 20d returns during periods of rising FEDFUNDS with statistical significance p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.0% and holds there for two consecutive months, the labor market is judged to be at or beyond full employment, raising wage-inflation risk and prompting expectations of continued monetary tightening, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in Oct 2005, near multi-year lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE sub-5.0% signals are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) across at least two distinct historical low-unemployment episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and continued rise in FEDFUNDS signals that higher borrowing costs are beginning to choke off housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumption weakness that transmits to equity markets with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2108K in Sep 2005, near cycle peak, while FEDFUNDS has risen steadily since mid-2004
regime_hint: housing boom peaking under monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX forward 60d returns show no significant negative association (Rank IC < 0.03) with joint HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise across at least one full tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals that higher rates are beginning to constrain industrial output, foreshadowing weaker corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.055 in Sep 2005 with FEDFUNDS at 3.78% amid ongoing hikes
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating industrial production
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during tightening regimes fails to precede negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 351561 million in Oct 2005, consistent with steady consumer spending growth
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5d SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly PAYEMS net gains fall below 100,000 for two consecutive reports, it signals an emerging slowdown in labor demand that precedes broader economic deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly net change < 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134,061K in Oct 2005, still showing moderate monthly job growth
failure_condition: Instances of sub-100K PAYEMS prints followed by non-negative mean 20d SPX returns exceed 50% of cases across a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates concurrently with rising FEDFUNDS, markets price in a longer and more aggressive tightening path, raising the equity risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 198.5 in Sep 2005 with FEDFUNDS at 3.78%, both trending upward amid post-Katrina energy price pressure
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS uptrends fail to produce significantly negative mean 60d SPX returns (p>0.05) across at least two historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-boom expansion phase, continued strength in GDPC1 growth alongside elevated HOUST levels signals broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this relationship reverses once housing activity turns down.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11193.2B in Q2 2005 with HOUST at 2108K, both indicating a still-expanding housing-linked economy
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1-HOUST co-movement fails to precede positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical housing-boom quarters, or the relationship shows no directional flip once HOUST declines for 2+ consecutive quarters.
